title: Measuring the knot of non-Hermitian degeneracies and non-commuting braids
data_sources: Cavity optomechanical susceptibility measurements (cryogenic, by request)

data_scope.system: Single cavity optomechanical device (N=3: two optical cavity modes + one mechanical mode)
data_scope.control_space: 4-dimensional parameter space Psi; 3-sphere hypersurface S scanned
data_scope.data_availability: by author request only; no public repository

core_conclusion: In non-Hermitian coupled-oscillator systems, closed control-parameter loops generically produce braids of eigenfrequencies, not trivial returns. For N > 2 oscillators these braids form a non-Abelian group whose structure is governed by a trefoil-knotted degeneracy locus in the three-sphere of control parameters. Demonstrated experimentally for N=3 via a cavity optomechanical system, establishing that non-commuting spectral braids are physically measurable.
why_it_matters: First general (arbitrary N) framework for spectral flow in non-Hermitian systems; discovers non-Abelian braiding in a table-top experiment; links knot theory to physical observables; opens pathway to non-Abelian topological operations in resonant devices and quantum hardware.
why_nature: Closes a long-standing N=2 limitation with a conceptually complete result; unifies braid-group mathematics with precision cryogenic experiment; discovers non-Abelian physics in an accessible platform; provides a transferable experimental toolkit for any multi-mode resonant system.
added_value_to_my_research: Active-measurement braid-inference -> active spatial sampling for geospatial foundation model fine-tuning with limited survey budgets
Non-Abelian braid encoding of urban mobility sequences -> order-sensitive topological descriptors for job-housing network analysis
Braid-group constraint as GNN inductive bias -> physics-informed graph learning for spatial accessibility modeling with non-commutative intervention effects
Spectral flow / catchment-reassignment analogy -> sequence-dependent policy analysis in 2SFCA/R2SFCA healthcare accessibility
GNN-accelerated knot reconstruction -> topology-preserving layout engine for spatial accessibility network visualization

figures.caption: Experimental schematic and susceptibility measurement — the cryogenic cavity optomechanical setup and how the complex eigenvalue spectrum λ is inferred from optical/microwave susceptibility.
Locating EP2 points on the hypersurface S — four independent signatures (discriminant zeroes and phase vortices, eigenvector indicator zeroes and vortices) mapping where two eigenvalues coalesce.
Measurements of the EP2 knot K and the eigenvalue braids — stereographic projections revealing the trefoil knot structure of the degeneracy locus, plus three distinct measured eigenvalue braids.
Non-commutation of control loops — the same pair of loops executed in reversed order produces different eigenvalue braids, the direct experimental proof of non-Abelian spectral flow.
